column2: Asset - Anything you own that has value.
Liability - Money you owe to others.
Equity - Ownership value in a business or asset.
Income - Money you earn.
Expense - Money you spend.
Budget - A plan for income and expenses.
Savings - Money kept aside for future use.
Investment - Putting money into something to help it grow.
Interest - Extra money paid for borrowing or earned from saving.
Inflation - Increase in prices over time.
Net Worth - Total assets minus total liabilities.
Credit Score - A number showing how reliable you are in repaying loans.
Loan - Borrowed money that must be repaid.
EMI - Fixed monthly loan repayment amount.
Collateral - Asset pledged as security for a loan.
Insurance - Protection against financial loss.
Premium - Amount paid regularly for insurance.
Deductible - Amount you pay before insurance coverage starts.
Revenue - Total income earned by a business.
Profit - Amount left after expenses are deducted from revenue.
Loss - When expenses exceed income.
Cash Flow - Movement of money in and out.
Dividend - Profit shared with shareholders.
Stock - A share representing ownership in a company.
Bond - A loan to a company/government with fixed interest.
Mutual Fund - A pool of money invested in stocks, bonds, etc.
SIP - Systematic monthly investment into mutual funds.
ETF - A fund traded like a stock on the exchange.
Portfolio - Collection of investments.
Risk - Chance of losing money.
Return - Profit earned from an investment.
Capital - Money used to start or run a business.
Depreciation - Decrease in asset value over time.
Appreciation - Increase in asset value over time.
Diversification - Spreading investments to reduce risk.
Market Value - Current price of an asset.
Liquidity - How easily an asset converts to cash.
Net Income - Income after expenses and taxes.
Gross Income - Income before any deductions.
Tax - Money paid to the government.
Tax Deduction - Reduction in taxable income.
Tax Exemption - Income not taxed.
Credit Limit - Maximum amount you can borrow.
Principal - Original amount invested or borrowed.
Compound Interest - Interest earned on interest.
Simple Interest - Interest earned only on principal.
Overdraft - Withdrawing more than available balance.
Bankruptcy - Legal state of being unable to pay debts.
Financial Literacy - Understanding financial concepts.
Cash Reserve - Emergency money kept aside.
Fixed Deposit - Deposit locked for a fixed time at interest.
Recurring Deposit - Monthly saving deposit in a bank.
Credit Card - Card allowing borrowing up to a limit.
Debit Card - Card using your bank balance for payments.
UPI - Instant mobile payment system.
NEFT - Bank-to-bank fund transfer system.
RTGS - High-value real-time transfer system.
IMPS - Instant fund transfer anytime.
KYC - Verification of customer identity.
Nominee - Person who receives assets after death.
Fund Manager - Person managing mutual fund investments.
Broker - Middleman for buying or selling shares.
Demat Account - Account holding shares electronically.
Trading Account - Account used to buy/sell shares.
Asset Allocation - Distribution of investments.
Bear Market - Market where prices fall.
Bull Market - Market where prices rise.
Expense Ratio - Fee charged by mutual funds.
Overnight Fund - Fund investing in one-day securities.
Credit Risk - Risk that borrower may not repay.
Market Risk - Risk due to market price changes.
Inflation Risk - Risk of returns losing value due to inflation.
IPO - First sale of a company's shares to the public.
NAV - Price per unit of a mutual fund.
Annuity - Regular payment for retirement.
Pension - Money paid after retirement.
Working Capital - Money for day-to-day business activities.
Break-even Point - Point where income equals expenses.
Asset Class - Category of investment.
Fiscal Year - Accounting year for taxation.
Audit - Examination of financial records.
Subsidy - Financial aid from the government.
Grant - Money given without repayment.
Royalty - Payment for using someone's work.
Copyright - Legal protection over creative work.
Net Banking - Online banking service.
Inflation Rate - Percentage change in prices.
CAGR - Yearly growth rate of an investment.
Balance Sheet - Statement of assets and liabilities.
Income Statement - Summary of income and expenses.
Transaction - Any financial activity.
Statement - Monthly summary of account activity.
Cheque - A written order for payment.
Bank Transfer - Moving money between accounts.
Fraud - Illegal financial deception.
Scam - Trick to steal money.
Overvaluation - Asset priced above true value.
Undervaluation - Asset priced below true value.
Hedge - Action taken to reduce investment risk.
Financial Goal - Target set for money planning.
